items.title: Introduction
items.description: How the guide works and what you'll build across 23 chapters.

items.title: Ch 1: Hello Agent
items.description: Get a conversational agent running in minutes with a streaming REPL.

items.title: Ch 2: Your First Tool
items.description: Define typed functions the agent can call and compose into actions.

items.title: Ch 3: Native Tools
items.description: 30 production-ready tools — web, filesystem, terminal, scheduling, and more.

items.title: Ch 4: MCP Servers
items.description: Connect any MCP-compatible tool server to your agent.

items.title: Ch 5: OpenAPI as Tools
items.description: Auto-generate agent tools from any OpenAPI 3.x spec.

items.title: Ch 6: Tool Sprawl & Custom Skills
items.description: Package tools into skills to cut context bloat and improve tool selection.

items.title: Ch 7: Native Skills
items.description: Expert workflows bundled and ready to drop in.

items.title: Ch 8: The Resource System
items.description: File-based project structure auto-loaded by the framework.

items.title: Ch 9: Custom Processors
items.description: Middleware-like hooks over every event in the agent lifecycle.

items.title: Ch 10: The Event System
items.description: The full event lifecycle, from message receipt to final response.

items.title: Ch 11: Debugging & Observability
items.description: Event stream inspection, cost tracking, processor-based export, and the events table.

items.title: Ch 12: RAG
items.description: Ground your agent on proprietary documents with semantic retrieval.

items.title: Ch 13: Knowledge Graph Collections
items.description: Model entities and relationships, not just similarity.

items.title: Ch 14: Graph Memory
items.description: Scalable agent memory that extracts entities and improves over time.

items.title: Ch 15: Assets
items.description: Move files, images, and binary data through the runtime without breaking the prompt.

items.title: Ch 16: Channels & The Server Facade
items.description: Ship to WhatsApp, web, Discord, and more via withApp.

items.title: Ch 17: Multi-Tenancy
items.description: Namespace isolation, per-tenant config, and scoped data access.

items.title: Ch 18: Collections — Application Data
items.description: Your business entities, stored in the same database as your agent.

items.title: Ch 19: Features — Custom Server Logic
items.description: Webhooks, admin APIs, and custom routes without a second server.

items.title: Ch 20: Multi-Agent Routing & Delegation
items.description: Build agent teams with routing, delegation, and loop prevention.

items.title: Ch 21: Goals — Automated Testing & Agent Simulation
items.description: Test agents with agents; score multi-turn conversations with a judge.

items.title: Ch 22: Custom LLM Providers
items.description: Plug in any model or provider the framework doesn't yet support.

items.title: Ch 23: What's Next
items.description: Web libraries, React UI, and where to go from here.
